purpose: Track a small set of active Complex mechanisms. Runtime behavior that belongs to the kernel, a boundary, or an engineering layer is not duplicated as a standalone mechanism.

normative_role_meanings.core: Normative behavior spine or responsibility/output boundary that every Complex run should respect.
normative_role_meanings.supporting_practice: A reusable practice that supports the core but is not itself a universal boundary.
normative_role_meanings.conditional_extension: An extension activated only when its matching project failure or scale condition appears.
normative_role_meanings.retired: No longer active; retained only when compatibility evidence requires it.

evidence_status_meanings.screened: The contract and external basis have been structurally reviewed, but no valid outcome comparison supports effectiveness.
evidence_status_meanings.tested: Behavior screens, filled examples, bounded pilots, or project observations exist, but real comparative validation is incomplete.
evidence_status_meanings.validated: Repeated, versioned real transcript or end-to-end comparisons show an outcome improvement in the stated scope.

promotion_requirements.screened_to_tested: Has a behavior case, transcript rule, landing point, implementation-grounded external basis, and a valid bounded outcome pilot that instantiates the claimed mechanism.
promotion_requirements.tested_to_validated: Has a locked case/scorer, baseline and candidate runs on the same task, candidate-preferring independent human review, and repeated evidence across at least two distinct real or redacted-real project sample sets.
promotion_requirements.normative_role_change: Changes only when project scope changes or repeated architecture evidence shows the mechanism is universal, supporting, conditional, or obsolete; it is not inferred from evidence status.
promotion_requirements.demotion: Evidence is not reproducible, the mechanism duplicates a stronger abstraction, Codex semantics changed, or observed overhead exceeds outcome value.

mechanisms.name: Complex behavior kernel
mechanisms.summary: The seven-behavior spine, four diagnostic layers, prompt rehydration, continuous cadence, target-function execution, forward-artifact acceptance, bounded recovery, and source resolution form one runtime kernel rather than separate gate families.
internal_evidence: The kernel is the single universal execution contract in README, quickstart, protocol/core, current-state, and the repo skill.
Prompt continuity, cadence, Loop completion, forward artifacts, source resolution, and four-layer diagnosis remain covered by behavior cases after standalone duplicate mechanisms were merged.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite completed six baseline and six candidate trials for queued execution and state reconciliation at 100% environment success in both arms. The ceiling supports contract executability, not superiority.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#behavior_kernel_end_to_end
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Already core because it is the compression layer for runtime execution, not a claim that every optional extension is validated.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If real runs show agents perform ceremony instead of the seven behaviors, simplify the kernel and move detail to tools or examples.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Any change to public entry, Codex surfaces, state recovery, continuation, or completion semantics.

mechanisms.name: Responsibility boundary
mechanisms.summary: AI owns project-internal decisions and continuation; user intervention, approval pauses, and resume safety are governed only by responsibility-bearing commitments and side effects.
internal_evidence: Repeated user feedback identifies unnecessary permission-seeking as a primary failure mode.
Behavior cases cover automatic continuation, credentials/external writes, interrupt-resume sequencing, and pointer-first intake.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite completed six baseline and six candidate trials for internal execution and exact publish-boundary handoff at 100% environment success in both arms; no differential signal appeared.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#responsibility_boundary_autonomy
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Already core because every run needs one authority and side-effect boundary.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If evidence shows systematic under-asking at real commitments or over-asking for internal work, revise the boundary definition rather than add another guard.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: New tools, approval surfaces, payment, publishing, external-write, or durable-resume semantics.

mechanisms.name: Delivery contract
mechanisms.summary: Audience, purpose, granularity, claim strength, and internal-information boundary determine delivery; routine observability remains minimum sufficient.
internal_evidence: Longstanding user feedback requires human-facing outputs to remain readable and separate from machine recovery state.
Behavior cases cover audience alignment and over-reporting.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite completed six baseline and six candidate trials for human-only and machine-recovery delivery at 100% environment success in both arms after local artifact precedence was applied.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#delivery_contract_audience_fit
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Already core because every run ends in either a reader-facing deliverable or a recoverable state transition.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: If output becomes too sparse for decisions or exposes internal machinery without reader value.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: A new audience, public claim class, or delivery surface appears.

mechanisms.name: Codex surface alignment
mechanisms.summary: Separates Codex primitives such as thread, turn, Goal, approval, skill, subagent, worktree, and automation from Complex conventions such as beat_objective and standing lane.
internal_evidence: Behavior cases cover Plan, Goal, cadence/heartbeat, subagents, and standing-lane boundaries.
Public wording now distinguishes Codex primitives from Complex conventions.

A real App task recorded create_goal and get_goal evidence while continuing its queued validation route; one writable codex-exec comparison completed both arms but could not expose Goal lifecycle events and was retained as insufficient evidence.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Validate only after versioned real runs across at least two Codex surfaces show fewer semantic errors.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: Codex semantics change or Complex conventions are again presented as platform primitives.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Codex app-server, Goal, Plan, approval, subagent, skill, thread, or automation changes.

mechanisms.name: Project nature router
mechanisms.summary: Selects evidence_fill, model_discovery, mixed, or execution_delivery weights without turning the labels into user-facing modes.
internal_evidence: Model-discovery and evidence-fill cases and examples exercise different routing weights.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Validate after paired end-to-end samples show fewer early-convergence and over-divergence errors under a locked evaluator.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: Agents over-label projects, stall execution, or cannot switch weights as evidence changes.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: A recurring project class does not fit the four current natures.

mechanisms.name: Independent review context separation
mechanisms.summary: True independent review uses a clean evaluator and bounded fact ledger; same-session review is labeled diagnostic.
internal_evidence: A behavior case and filled example distinguish diagnostic self-review from independent evaluation.

A 2026-07-17 paired fixture comparison ran three trials per arm but both persuasive-framing and clean-framing arms reached 100% recall and precision. Independent review was not actually instantiated across sessions, so the record is revise_and_repeat rather than promotion evidence.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Validate after paired clean-context and same-session reviews show better defect finding without unacceptable recovery loss.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: The isolation cost exceeds defect-detection value for the stated task class.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Reviewer, subagent, thread, context-compaction, or eval tooling changes.

mechanisms.name: Behavior transcript review
mechanisms.summary: Marker rules screen transcripts; versioned eval cases, runs, scorers, and human outcomes determine comparison and maturity claims.
internal_evidence: Behavior and transcript inventories are structurally checked.
The active re-baseline identifies a single structured eval record as the next evidence requirement; marker pass remains a screen, not validation.

Three repeated baseline/candidate fixture comparisons now use opaque sample IDs, content-hashed conditions, deterministic scores, compact result pointers, and conservative insufficient-evidence or revise-and-repeat decisions.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Validate only after locked same-task comparisons link the mechanism to lower correction cost or better outcomes.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: Marker pass repeatedly disagrees with human outcome judgment.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Every real transcript batch, scorer change, or eval schema change.

mechanisms.name: Operating organization
mechanisms.summary: Recurring multi-lane work may establish controller, human interface, evidence/data, model/component, data-code, review/risk, and delivery responsibilities before local optimization.
internal_evidence: Behavior cases and examples define recurring-lane and clean-review contracts.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite completed six baseline and six candidate trials for recurring lanes and a one-off negative control at 100% environment success in both arms. The result establishes executability but no measured lane benefit.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#operating_organization_lane_value
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Move to tested only after a discriminating bounded comparison shows lane value without one-off overhead. Validate only after two distinct real or redacted-real multi-lane comparisons reduce correction load, duplicate work, or artifact lead time and pass blind human review.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: Lane records or platform resources consume more work than they coordinate.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Recurring responsibility, reviewer contamination, topology drift, or Codex multi-agent semantics change.

mechanisms.name: Portfolio control plane
mechanisms.summary: Multi-module projects may use one authoritative target function, module/lane portfolio, route state, branch parking, and stop condition; ordinary projects do not need this extension.
internal_evidence: Cases and examples cover local-greedy routing and portfolio state.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite completed six baseline and six candidate trials for a local blocker and a true global boundary at 100% environment success in both arms. It instantiates the route contract but gives no evidence of advantage over native behavior.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#portfolio_blocked_branch_routing
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Move to tested only after a discriminating multi-module shadow comparison improves routing without schema or bookkeeping regressions. Validate only after repeated real comparisons reduce false stops, duplicate work, or artifact latency and pass blind human review.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: Bookkeeping becomes a substitute for execution or no longer changes routing.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Module/lane expansion, repeated local-greedy failure, or orchestration surface change.

mechanisms.name: Trace appraisal and Hot/Warm/Cold state
mechanisms.summary: Long projects may keep a short Hot State, compact Warm indexes, and cold trace pointers when context pressure is demonstrated.
internal_evidence: Behavior cases and one filled example exist.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite completed six baseline and six candidate trials for oversized-state recovery and a small-state negative control at 100% environment success in both arms. It verifies exact recovery and non-overapplication but cannot measure long-project context value.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#trace_appraisal_oversized_recovery
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Move to tested only after a discriminating context-pressure comparison improves recovery or context cost without over-tiering small state. Validate only after repeated long-project paired runs preserve semantic recovery and pass blind human review.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: Compaction hides the current basis, weakens handoff, or creates archive ceremony.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Measured context growth, failed recovery, or changed memory/checkpoint implementation.

mechanisms.name: External reference transfer discipline
mechanisms.summary: Mechanism-level changes inspect pinned implementations, reproduce bounded behavior when meaningful, compare on the same task, transfer reversibly, and separate upstream evidence from Complex validation.
internal_evidence: Twelve pinned reference implementations are recorded; two bounded upstream fixtures are reproduced.
The active architecture re-baseline applies keep/merge/demote decisions to every remaining mechanism and four repository-wide surfaces.

A seven-experiment program gives every core and conditional-extension mechanism a falsifiable claim, implementation-level external basis, frozen contract and writable samples, repeated arms, environment/trajectory/human graders, validity threats, and an explicit migration rule.

The provenance-bound 2026-07-17 full suite and append-only rescore completed six baseline and six candidate trials for misleading similarity and a locked negative comparison at 100% environment success in both arms. The rescore corrected one semantically valid nested JSON path. This supports contract executability but no promotion beyond screened.
eval_result:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T145657Z-4db18e9646-gpt-5.6-luna.json#external_calibration_decision_quality
eval_rescore:docs/evals/results/executable-pilots-20260717T171657Z-a091c06076-rescore.json#external_calibration_decision_quality
No same-task real Complex transfer is yet validated.
mechanisms.promotion_rule: Move to tested only after a discriminating same-task transfer comparison changes a decision or outcome without citation ceremony. Validate only after at least two reversible transfers improve locked real-project outcomes and pass independent blind human review.
mechanisms.demotion_trigger: The process becomes citation ceremony, implementation evidence is skipped, or comparison cost does not change decisions.
mechanisms.refresh_trigger: Mechanism-level change, upstream revision, stale assumption, contradictory run, or maturity promotion.
